title: Encore Fellowships in the United States | $25,000 Paid Fellowship for Experienced Professionals
summary: Are you an experienced professional looking to use your expertise to create meaningful social impact? The Encore Fellowships program offers accomplished professionals an opportunity to transition their decades of experience into nonprofit leadership while earning a $25,000 stipend . Designed for professionals with at least 20 years of work experience , Encore Fellowships connect skilled individuals with nonprofit organizations that need experienced leaders to strengthen their operations, build organizational capacity, and expand their impact. Fellows work on strategic projects while gaining valuable exposure to the nonprofit sector and making a lasting difference in their communities. If you are passionate about using your professional background for meaningful change, the Encore Fellowship could be the ideal next step in your career. About Encore Fellowships Encore Fellowships are designed to bridge the gap between experienced professionals and nonprofit organizations seeking high-level expertise. Rather than filling traditional employment positions, Fellows are matched with organizations that require strategic guidance, leadership, and specialized skills for capacity-building initiatives. Each Fellow works closely with a nonprofit organization over a period of 6 to 12 months , contributing approximately 1,000 hours —typically around 20 hours per week , although some placements may require up to 35 hours weekly. Participants receive professional support while helping nonprofit organizations strengthen their operations and increase long-term sustainability. Fellowship Benefits Selected Fellows will receive numerous professional and personal benefits, including: $25,000 compensation for their service Opportunity to work on high-impact nonprofit projects Collaborate directly with nonprofit leadership teams Apply decades of professional expertise to meaningful causes Expand knowledge of the nonprofit and social impact sector Develop new leadership experiences Build professional networks within mission-driven organizations Flexible work arrangements depending on placement Remote Hybrid Onsite Fellowship Structure Successful Fellows will: Contribute approximately 1,000 hours Serve for 6–12 months Work approximately 20–35 hours each week Support nonprofit organizations on strategic capacity-building projects Be employed and paid through Encore Fellowships, which manages: Candidate screening Onboarding Payroll administration Employment logistics Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Have 20 or more years of paid professional work experience Demonstrate a strong interest in supporting nonprofit or social impact organizations Be able to commit approximately 1,000 hours over 6–12 months Be legally eligible to work in the United States Be available to work remotely, onsite, or in a hybrid arrangement Possess significant professional expertise that can benefit nonprofit organizations Ideal Candidates Encore Fellowships are particularly suitable for professionals who have served in senior or leadership positions such as: Executive Directors Marketing Directors Communications Directors Digital Transformation Leaders Operations Managers Human Resource Leaders Finance Professionals IT Specialists Program Managers Strategy Consultants Business Executives Project Managers Professionals from both the private and public sectors are encouraged to express their interest. Areas of Expertise Sought Encore Fellowships welcome professionals with expertise in areas including: Marketing Communications Brand Strategy Fundraising Digital Marketing Analytics Social Media Technology Digital Transformation Human Resources Finance Operations Project Management Leadership Development Organizational Strategy Capacity Building Work Location Options Depending on the nonprofit organization s needs, Fellows may work: Remotely Onsite Hybrid Who Can Apply? Applications are currently open to experienced professionals who: Reside in the United States Are eligible to work in the United States Meet the professional experience requirements At this time, the program is only available in the United States . Application Process Interested applicants should complete the online interest form and provide: Personal contact information Work experience Preferred work location LinkedIn profile Previous leadership positions Areas of expertise Previous industry experience Additional comments about their interests Following submission, an Encore Fellowship Manager will contact eligible candidates to discuss suitable fellowship opportunities. Required Information Applicants should prepare: First and last name Telephone number Email address City and State ZIP Code LinkedIn Profile Years of paid work experience Previous job titles Core professional expertise Industry background Work location preference General comments Why Apply? Encore Fellowships provide a unique opportunity to transition decades of professional experience into meaningful social impact. Instead of stepping away from leadership after a successful career, Fellows continue making a difference by helping nonprofit organizations solve real-world challenges. The fellowship allows professionals to use their expertise in strategy, operations, communications, technology, finance, and many other fields while receiving financial compensation and expanding their professional networks. It is an excellent opportunity for experienced professionals who want to leave a lasting legacy through service. How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official Encore Fellowships interest form. Application Portal: https://fedcap.tfaforms.net/5178761?tfa_Ownerid=005G000000641Ng tfa_9504653290883=001Qn00000SxnZQ For questions regarding the application process: Email: efinfo@fedcap.org Conclusion The Encore Fellowships program offers experienced professionals an exceptional opportunity to leverage decades of expertise in service of nonprofit organizations across the United States. With a $25,000 paid fellowship , flexible work arrangements, and meaningful capacity-building projects, the program enables accomplished professionals to continue making a significant impact while supporting organizations dedicated to social good. If you have over 20 years of professional experience and are passionate about giving back through your skills and leadership, this fellowship is well worth exploring. title: Institute for Youth in Policy Public Policy Fellowship 2026 (Fall Cohort): Apply for a Selective U.S. Leadership Programme in Public Policy, Research & Civic Engagement
summary: Applications are open for the Institute for Youth in Policy Public Policy Fellowship 2026 Fall Cohort , an intensive two-month development programme for young people interested in public policy, civic engagement and social impact. The fellowship gives selected students opportunities to examine contemporary political, social, economic and environmental issues while developing practical skills in policy research, analysis, writing, communication, teamwork and project management. Fellows participate in seminars, debates, workshops, speaker sessions and collaborative policy activities. They also develop an original individual capstone project and work with peers on a nonpartisan policy brief. Applications for the Fall 2026 cohort close on 19 August 2026 . Decisions are expected to be released on 26 August 2026 , while the official programme website lists the cohort dates as 12 September to 7 November 2026 . About the Public Policy Fellowship The Public Policy Fellowship is organised by the Institute for Youth in Policy , a youth-focused organisation working in public policy, civic education, discourse and political engagement. The programme is designed to help emerging leaders understand how public policy is researched, developed, communicated and debated. Rather than focusing exclusively on lectures, the fellowship combines academic learning with collaborative and practical assignments. Participants explore policy from personal, local and broader national or global perspectives. They work with fellow students, programme mentors, YIP department executives, guest speakers and other stakeholders to examine current policy challenges and propose evidence-informed responses. Duration and Fall 2026 Dates The fellowship runs for approximately two months. The official 2026 schedule lists four cohorts: Winter: 17 January–21 March 2026 Spring: 11 April–6 June 2026 Summer: 20 June–22 August 2026 Fall: 12 September–7 November 2026 The Fall cohort application deadline is 19 August 2026 , and admissions decisions are scheduled for 26 August 2026 . Applications close at 11:59 p.m. PST on the stated deadline date. A recent promotional announcement states that the Fall cohort ends on 8 November. However, the official fellowship webpage currently lists 7 November 2026 . Applicants should follow the dates communicated in their acceptance materials if selected. What Fellows Will Learn The fellowship is structured to help young participants develop skills relevant to public policy, civic leadership, research and advocacy. Participants can expect to strengthen their abilities in: Public policy research Policy analysis Nonpartisan thinking Research and analytical writing Strategic communication Public speaking and presentation Debate and constructive discourse Teamwork Project management Problem-solving Leadership Community and stakeholder engagement The programme encourages fellows to consider complex policy problems from different perspectives rather than approaching them through a single political or ideological position. Programme Activities Selected fellows participate in several interconnected activities throughout the programme. Interactive Seminars and Workshops Fellows attend sessions designed to introduce policy concepts, research methods and approaches to civic engagement. Workshops may focus on subjects such as policy development, political communication, social impact, advocacy, research writing and project planning. Policy Discussions and Debates Thought-provoking discussions allow participants to examine policy challenges, hear different perspectives and practise communicating their ideas clearly. The emphasis on nonpartisan discussion is intended to help fellows engage respectfully with disagreement and analyse issues beyond political slogans. Guest Speaker Sessions Each cohort normally includes two speaker sessions with professionals or experts working in different policy fields. These sessions give fellows opportunities to learn about real-world policy work and ask questions about public service, advocacy, leadership and research. Collaborative Policy Brief Fellows work with their peers to research, write and present an original nonpartisan policy brief. This assignment provides practical experience in: Identifying a policy problem Conducting background research Evaluating evidence Comparing possible policy responses Developing recommendations Writing for a policy audience Presenting findings clearly Individual Capstone Project Every fellow is also expected to develop an individual capstone project. The capstone begins during the early part of the programme and continues until the end of the fellowship. Completed projects may be published on the Institute for Youth in Policy website. Previous fellowship topics have addressed areas such as social change, education, public-policy reform, urban renewal and technology-related initiatives. Weekly Schedule and Time Commitment The programme is designed to accommodate students with school, university and other responsibilities. According to the official fellowship page, participants attend two mandatory synchronous meetings each week: Wednesdays at 8:00 p.m. EST Saturdays at 12:00 p.m. EST Fellows generally spend approximately four to six hours per week on meetings, research, policy writing, workshops and independent capstone work. Applicants should convert the meeting times carefully and confirm the applicable time zone when the programme begins, particularly because seasonal clock changes can affect local meeting times. Policy Areas Covered The fellowship takes a cross-sector approach. Participants may examine issues connected to: Social and political change Education Criminal justice Economic policy Environmental policy Urban renewal Public-policy reform Community development Technology and SMART infrastructure Civic engagement Advocacy Political institutions The exact subjects may vary according to the cohort, speakers, current events and fellows’ capstone interests. Mentorship and Professional Development Fellows receive ongoing guidance throughout the programme. Mentorship and small-group support can help participants improve their research, refine their policy arguments and develop clearer professional goals. The fellowship also provides opportunities to connect with: Other students interested in policy YIP programme leaders Experienced mentors Policy and education professionals Fellowship alumni Guest speakers The Institute reports that more than 1,000 fellows have graduated, over 300 capstones have been published and fellowship projects have received approximately 3.2 million views. It also reports an average acceptance rate of 5–8%, indicating a selective admissions process. What Happens After the Fellowship? Completing the programme may provide participants with a published research or policy project that can be added to an academic or professional portfolio. Fellowship graduates may also receive priority consideration for selected positions within the Institute for Youth in Policy. The organisation states that alumni can access a priority hiring pipeline and opportunities to connect with senior YIP leadership. However, completing the fellowship does not guarantee employment or appointment to another YIP position. Who Can Apply? The official fellowship website currently states that applicants must be: High school or undergraduate students Between 13 and 23 years old Interested or experienced in public policy, civic engagement, advocacy, social impact, community organising, design thinking or constructive discourse Based in the United States Able to participate in meetings scheduled around Eastern, Central and Pacific U.S. time zones Graduate students and people who have already graduated are not eligible. The official website also states that the programme is not open to international applicants because the workshops focus substantially on U.S. public policy and political systems. Important Eligibility Discrepancy A recent promotional post describes the Fall fellowship as open to students aged 16–25 and refers to collaboration with peers and mentors from around the world. However, the official fellowship page currently states: Ages 13–23 High school and undergraduate students only No graduate students or graduates No international applicants Because these details conflict, prospective applicants should rely on the current official application form or contact the fellowship team directly at fellowship@yipinstitute.org before applying, especially if they are aged 24–25 or based outside the United States. How to Apply Applicants must complete the online fellowship application form. The application will likely ask candidates to explain their interests, background, motivation and experience related to policy or civic engagement. Applicants should prepare to discuss: Why they are interested in public policy A policy issue they care about Their experience in leadership, advocacy or community engagement What they hope to learn How they would contribute to the cohort Their ability to meet the weekly time commitment APPLY HERE Tips for a Strong Application A strong application should be focused, specific and personal. Applicants should: Identify one or two policy issues they genuinely care about. Explain why those issues matter to their community or personal experience. Provide examples of leadership, research, volunteering or civic participation. Demonstrate curiosity and willingness to engage with different perspectives. Explain how the fellowship supports their academic or career goals. Avoid general statements that could apply to every candidate. Proofread the application before submission. Candidates do not necessarily need an extensive policy résumé. A thoughtful explanation of their interests, initiative and potential contribution may be more persuasive than a parade of impressive-sounding titles wearing tiny hats. Application Deadline Applications for the Fall Public Policy Fellowship close on: 19 August 2026 at 11:59 p.m. PST Admissions decisions are scheduled to be released on: 26 August 2026 The official Fall cohort dates are: 12 September–7 November 2026 Applicants should submit early and verify that they have received confirmation that their application was successfully recorded. Conclusion The Institute for Youth in Policy Public Policy Fellowship 2026 offers high school and undergraduate students an opportunity to gain practical experience in policy research, analytical writing, debate, communication and civic leadership. Through workshops, mentorship, collaborative policy briefs, speaker events and an individual capstone project, fellows can develop tangible work while joining a selective community of young people interested in public affairs. Eligible students should carefully review the official requirements, confirm that they can attend the two weekly meetings and submit their applications by 19 August 2026 at 11:59 p.m. PST . For more information about this opportunity, CLICK HERE . To discover more fellowship opportunities, CLICK HERE . title: GEF SGP Nigeria Call for Proposals 2026: Strategic Grant to Host the Western & Eastern Anglophone Africa Regional Capacity-Building Workshop
summary: GEF SGP Nigeria is inviting qualified organisations to submit proposals to organise and deliver the Western and Eastern Anglophone Africa Regional Capacity-Building Workshop 2026. The regional workshop is expected to take place in Abuja, Nigeria, in November 2026 and bring together approximately 35 participants from participating African countries. Attendees are expected to include National Coordinators, former and current grantees, National Steering Committee representatives, UNDP Country Office representatives, the SGP Regional Focal Point and members of the SGP Central Programme Management Team. The selected organisation will manage the workshop’s travel, accommodation, subsistence, conference arrangements, field visit, visibility materials and knowledge products. The grant provides funding of up to US$100,000 , while applicants must demonstrate secured co-financing of at least the same amount in cash or in kind. The deadline for submitting proposals is 27 July 2026 . About the GEF SGP Regional Workshop The Global Environment Facility (GEF) Small Grants Programme (SGP) supports community-led initiatives that address environmental challenges while contributing to sustainable development. The planned workshop will provide a regional platform for countries participating in the Small Grants Programme to examine results achieved during Operational Phase 7 and prepare for continued implementation under Operational Phase 8. Participants will discuss achievements, challenges, lessons learned, impact stories and future opportunities for collaboration and resource mobilisation. The call states that participants will come from 12 countries, but the announcement lists 11: Cape Verde Eritrea Ethiopia The Gambia Ghana Liberia Nigeria Rwanda Sierra Leone Tanzania Uganda Prospective applicants should consult the official proposal documents for clarification regarding the final list of participating countries. Main Objectives of the Regional Workshop The Western and Eastern Anglophone Africa Regional Capacity-Building Workshop is intended to support reflection, learning and future planning across participating GEF SGP country programmes. Its main objectives include the following. Review Operational Phase 7 Implementation Participants will review progress under Operational Phase 7, including: Landscape approaches Country-level targets Results achieved Challenges encountered Lessons learned Good practices developed at country and project levels Environmental and community impact stories This review will help participants identify what worked, what needs improvement and what lessons should guide future programme implementation. Discuss GEF-8 Priorities The workshop will create space for National Coordinators and other stakeholders to discuss the strategic objectives and wider vision of GEF-8. These discussions are expected to help participating countries understand how their national and regional activities connect with the current priorities of the Global Environment Facility. Assess the Transition to Operational Phase 8 Participants will review progress concerning the transition to UNDP and implementation under Operational Phase 8. The discussions may cover: Operational arrangements Administrative processes Grant-making procedures Common implementation problems Potential regional solutions Opportunities for improved coordination Promote Peer Learning A field visit to a selected SGP-supported project will allow participants to observe community-level implementation and exchange practical experience. The visit is expected to support peer learning by showing how environmental initiatives are managed in practice and how communities contribute to project results. Explore Future Resource Mobilisation The workshop will examine opportunities to strengthen the long-term sustainability of the Small Grants Programme. Topics are expected to include: GEF-9 STAR negotiations at country level Partnerships with the private sector Resource mobilisation Replication of successful initiatives Scaling up effective projects Transboundary initiatives across the African region Sustainability of community-based environmental action Strengthen Communication of SGP Results Another major objective is to improve how the programme communicates its results and impact. The selected organisation will support the production of a documentary and publication highlighting projects from participating countries. These materials should present: Project results Impact stories Lessons learned Good practices Community experiences Examples of successful environmental interventions Scope of Work for the Selected Organisation The successful organisation will be responsible for coordinating the workshop before, during and after the event. 1. Organise the Regional Workshop in Abuja The organisation will plan and deliver one Africa subregional workshop for approximately 35 participants. The grant is expected to cover: International and domestic airfares Accommodation Subsistence costs Conference packages Workshop venue arrangements Participant coordination Pre-event logistics On-site logistical support Post-event follow-up The selected organisation will work with the SGP Nigeria office throughout the planning and implementation process. 2. Organise a Project Site Visit The organisation must arrange a visit to an SGP-selected project. Relevant costs may include: Transport hire Accommodation where required Meals and subsistence Coordination with the host project Participant logistics Field-visit documentation 3. Produce a Video Documentary A video documentary must be produced to record the project site visit and communicate the work and impact of the Small Grants Programme. The documentary should help present community-level achievements in a format that can be used for learning, visibility and outreach. 4. Produce a Regional Publication The organisation will prepare a publication featuring projects presented by participating countries. The publication should document: Project results Environmental and community impact Lessons learned Individual project case studies Replicable practices Examples of innovation Regional experiences under Operational Phase 7 5. Produce Workshop Materials The selected organisation will be responsible for producing relevant workshop and visibility materials, including: T-shirts Pens Jackets Reusable or biodegradable bags Other approved workshop materials Where possible, materials should reflect environmental sustainability principles. 6. Prepare the Final Workshop Report A comprehensive report must be produced following the workshop. The report should capture: Workshop proceedings Main discussion points Presentations Lessons learned Recommendations Agreements and action points Field-visit findings Opportunities for future collaboration Complete reports must be submitted no later than two weeks after the regional workshop. Expected Outputs The successful applicant will be expected to deliver the following outputs: Africa subregional workshop report Regional publication of GEF SGP impact stories Individual project case studies Video documentary covering the project site visit Workshop and visibility materials Evidence of successful workshop implementation Documentation of lessons, recommendations and follow-up actions Maximum Grant Amount The maximum grant available is: Up to US$100,000 The final amount awarded may depend on the quality of the proposal, approved activities, budget justification and the assessment of the applicant’s capacity. Applicants should prepare a realistic and detailed budget covering the activities required under the terms of reference. Co-Financing Requirement Applicants must provide evidence of secured co-financing worth at least: US$100,000 The contribution may be provided in: Cash In-kind support A combination of cash and in-kind contributions Applicants should clearly explain the source, amount and status of each contribution. In-kind contributions may include eligible staff time, equipment, facilities, services or other resources directly supporting programme delivery, subject to the official rules. A vague promise of future support is unlikely to be enough. The call specifically requires evidence that commensurate co-financing has been secured. Project Duration The project will run for four months , beginning in July 2026. The regional workshop itself is expected to take place in November 2026. Final reports must be submitted no later than two weeks after the workshop. Applicants should provide a practical implementation plan covering: Preparatory activities Participant and travel coordination Workshop delivery Field-visit arrangements Knowledge-product development Reporting and financial closure Eligibility Criteria Applicants must satisfy all the stated eligibility requirements. Eligible organisations must: Be previous GEF Small Grants Programme grantees Have experience organising international workshops Demonstrate experience in strategic knowledge management Have experience managing SGP grants valued at no less than US$50,000 Demonstrate experience in partnership building Demonstrate experience in resource mobilisation Have sufficient organisational and financial capacity Be able to secure the required co-financing Be able to complete all activities and reports within the project period The announcement explicitly advises organisations not to contact the programme unless they meet every listed criterion. What Could Strengthen an Application? A competitive proposal should provide strong evidence rather than broad claims. Applicants should consider including: Examples of international workshops previously organised References from former partners Evidence of completed SGP-funded projects Financial records showing experience managing grants above US$50,000 Examples of publications, documentaries or knowledge products A clear logistics plan A detailed risk-management strategy Confirmed co-financing documentation A realistic work plan Relevant staff biographies Evidence of partnership-building experience A clear approach to environmental sustainability The budget should be consistent with the work plan and should clearly distinguish grant-funded costs from co-financing. How to Apply Interested and eligible organisations should request the official proposal template by contacting: ibironke-favour.olubamise@undp.org Applicants should use the required template and follow all instructions provided by the GEF SGP Nigeria office. Only shortlisted organisations will be contacted. Application Deadline The deadline for submitting proposals is: 27 July 2026 The announcement does not specify a closing time or time zone. Applicants should submit before the final day to avoid uncertainty or technical delays. Conclusion The GEF SGP Nigeria Call for Proposals 2026 offers an experienced former Small Grants Programme grantee the opportunity to organise a major regional capacity-building workshop in Abuja. The selected organisation will manage a complex assignment involving international travel, accommodation, conference logistics, a field visit, documentary production, regional publication development and final reporting. With grant funding of up to US$100,000 and a matching co-financing requirement of at least US$100,000, applicants must demonstrate significant institutional experience, financial capacity and knowledge-management expertise. Eligible organisations should request the official proposal template, prepare a detailed application and submit it by 27 July 2026 . For further information and application requirements, CLICK HERE. To discover more grants opportunities, VISIT HERE. title: World Bank Treasury Summer Internship 2027: Paid Finance Internship in Washington, DC with Visa Sponsorship for International and U.S. College Students
summary: The World Bank Treasury Summer Internship 2027 is now accepting applications from outstanding undergraduate students who aspire to build careers in finance, investment banking, capital markets, treasury operations, asset management, sustainable finance, and international development. As the only internship programme within the World Bank dedicated specifically to college students , this highly competitive opportunity provides participants with practical experience at the intersection of global finance, innovation, and development. Hosted by the World Bank Treasury , the internship offers far more than a traditional summer work experience. Interns become part of one of the world s most respected public-sector financial institutions, working alongside experts responsible for managing hundreds of billions of dollars in assets, supporting global financial markets, and financing development projects that improve lives worldwide. Throughout the 10-week programme, participants rotate across multiple Treasury teams, receive mentorship from experienced professionals, attend technical and career development sessions, network with senior leaders, and complete a capstone project. The internship also serves as the primary recruitment pathway into the prestigious World Bank Treasury Junior Analyst Programme , making it an outstanding opportunity for students planning long-term careers in finance. Applications for the World Bank Treasury Summer Internship 2027 are currently open. About the World Bank Treasury The World Bank Treasury (WBG Treasury) is the treasury department of the World Bank Group and serves as one of the world s leading public-sector treasury organizations. Reporting directly to the Managing Director and World Bank Group Chief Financial Officer, Treasury performs treasury and financial management functions for: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD) International Development Association (IDA) International Finance Corporation (IFC) Other World Bank Group entities Selected public- and private-sector organizations For more than 80 years, Treasury has earned a reputation as a prudent borrower, innovative investor, and trusted risk manager in international financial markets. Its responsibilities include: Managing approximately US$300 billion in assets for the World Bank Group and other official-sector investors. Leading the Triple-A-rated financing programmes of IBRD, IDA, and IFC in international and domestic bond and derivative markets. Conducting asset and liability management for the balance sheets of IBRD, IDA, and IFC. Developing financing and hedging products for developing-country clients. Supporting World Bank member countries through advisory services that strengthen financial market capacity. Working closely with finance, operations, investment, and development teams across the World Bank Group. This broad scope gives interns unparalleled exposure to global financial markets while contributing to the World Bank s mission of supporting sustainable development. About the World Bank Group The World Bank Group is one of the world s largest sources of development finance, technical expertise, and research. The organization consists of five institutions working together to reduce poverty, promote shared prosperity, and support sustainable development across the globe. Key facts include: 189 member countries More than 120 offices worldwide Partnerships with governments, international organizations, and private-sector institutions Investments in projects that address some of the world s most pressing development challenges By joining the Treasury Summer Internship, participants gain insight into how global finance contributes to economic growth and development. About the World Bank Treasury Summer Internship The World Bank Treasury Summer Internship is a junior professional training programme designed specifically for college students with strong academic records and a demonstrated interest in finance. The programme prepares students for careers in: Capital markets Investment banking Asset management Treasury operations Financial technology Sustainable finance International finance Development finance Risk management Unlike many internships that focus on observation, participants actively contribute to Treasury projects while receiving structured professional training and mentorship. The internship combines: Hands-on work experience Treasury team rotations Technical finance training Leadership development Networking opportunities Executive engagement Capstone presentations Career preparation The programme also lays the foundation for future employment through the World Bank Treasury Junior Analyst Programme. Internship Duration The internship lasts 10 weeks and will take place from: 1 June 2027 to 9 August 2027 Participants are expected to: Work full-time Attend in person Be based in Washington, DC Participate throughout the entire programme Official Internship Calendar The programme follows a structured schedule: Onboarding Week 1 June – 4 June 2027 Rotation One 7 June – 25 June 2027 Rotation Two 28 June – 16 July 2027 Rotation Three 19 July – 6 August 2027 Capstone Presentation 9 August 2027 Observed Holidays 18 June 2027 5 July 2027 Internship Structure One of the defining features of the World Bank Treasury Summer Internship is its rotational learning model. Following onboarding, interns rotate through three different Treasury teams , spending approximately three weeks with each. This allows participants to: Experience different areas of Treasury operations. Develop a broader understanding of international financial markets. Work with professionals across multiple business functions. Explore different career paths within Treasury. Throughout the internship, participants also engage in: Weekly discussions with the Programme Director. Technical learning sessions. Career-focused workshops. Meetings with Treasury officers, managers, and directors. Networking events with Treasury professionals. Opportunities to meet the World Bank Vice President and Treasurer. Each rotation concludes with a presentation to the host team, while the internship ends with a capstone presentation delivered to Treasury management. Treasury Departments and Teams Treasury expects to recruit up to 17 interns across its 17 teams , with each intern following a unique rotation schedule. The main Treasury departments include: Capital Markets Teams include: CMDDO (Debt Origination) CMDIS (Investor Relations Sustainable Finance) Asset Management Including: TREAM (Treasury Asset Management) Treasury Client Solutions Structuring Including: TREAB (Asset Liability and Balance Sheet Management) TREFP (Financial Products) Treasury Partnerships Advisory Including: TREAP (Partnerships Engagements) TREPK (Advisory Knowledge) Treasury Operations Including: TREFT (Financial Technology) The World Bank notes that hiring teams and organizational needs may change over time. Salary and Benefits The World Bank Treasury Summer Internship is a paid internship . Interns work a total of 400 hours under a short-term temporary contract. Compensation includes: US Citizens: US$27.70 per hour (gross) Non-US Citizens: US$22.70 per hour (net) Additional benefits include: A World Bank-issued laptop. Structured professional training. Mentorship throughout the programme. Networking with Treasury professionals. Exposure to senior leadership. Visa sponsorship for eligible international participants. Visa Sponsorship The World Bank sponsors the appropriate U.S. work visa for successful international applicants who require authorization to work in the United States. This enables qualified students from outside the United States to participate fully in the internship. Mentorship and Professional Development Professional development is an integral part of the internship. Each participant is assigned: A mentor A rotation coordinator Mentors provide guidance on: Daily work assignments Career planning Technical development Professional communication Workplace expectations Interns also gain access to a Treasury community of nearly 500 professionals , creating valuable networking and learning opportunities. Capstone Project The internship concludes with a capstone project presented to Treasury management. This final presentation enables interns to demonstrate: Analytical thinking Financial knowledge Communication skills Research ability Professional growth Participants also present their work at the conclusion of each departmental rotation. Pathway to the Treasury Junior Analyst Programme The internship serves as the primary pathway into the World Bank Treasury Junior Analyst Programme . Following successful completion of the internship, selected participants may receive the opportunity to apply for Junior Analyst positions. Applications for Junior Analyst roles open after the internship concludes. Junior Analyst positions: Begin after graduation. Are awarded through a competitive selection process. Offer a one-year appointment. May be renewed for one additional year, for a maximum of two years. Potential hiring teams include: Capital Markets Debt Origination Investor Relations Sustainable Finance Treasury Asset Management Asset Liability and Balance Sheet Management Financial Products Partnerships Engagements Advisory Knowledge Financial Technology Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements: Be enrolled in the second-to-last year of the equivalent of a four-year college degree programme. Demonstrate an interest in finance, business, economics, or a related discipline. Be expected to graduate between December 2027 and September 2028 . Be in good academic standing. Be available to work full-time for the entire internship. Not have another employment contract or internship during the programme. Applicants found to have misrepresented information may be disqualified or dismissed. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: One-page PDF cover letter. One-page PDF résumé. Optional Document Applicants may also submit: A maximum half-page PDF personal statement. The optional statement allows applicants to explain: Their background. Personal experiences. Perspectives. How they would contribute to Treasury s diverse workplace. Although optional, it offers an opportunity to strengthen the application. Diversity and Inclusion The World Bank Treasury values diversity and inclusion and considers them central to its success. Treasury employees represent: More than 60 nationalities. Staff are located in offices including: Washington Paris Tokyo Singapore Chennai Sofia The World Bank is an equal opportunity employer and states that it does not discriminate based on: Gender Race Ethnicity Religion Disability Sexual orientation Gender identity The organization emphasizes that excellence and opportunity should be accessible regardless of background. Evaluation and Selection Process Applications submitted through the official online portal undergo a structured review process. The recruitment process includes: Review of complete applications by the recruitment committee. Remote assessment for selected candidates. Interviews with shortlisted applicants. Final selection. According to the World Bank, all applicants will be notified of the outcome by December 2026 . How to Apply Interested applicants must complete the official online application through the World Bank Group careers portal . Applicants should prepare: A one-page cover letter. A one-page résumé. An optional personal statement. The World Bank also encourages applicants to use its suggested résumé and cover letter templates before submission. Why You Should Apply The World Bank Treasury Summer Internship offers a rare opportunity to launch a career in global finance while contributing to international development. Participants will benefit from: Paid professional work experience. Competitive hourly compensation. Exposure to global financial markets. Rotations across multiple Treasury departments. Technical and leadership development. Mentorship from experienced finance professionals. Networking opportunities with senior Treasury leaders. A capstone presentation to Treasury management. Visa sponsorship for eligible international students. A pathway into the World Bank Treasury Junior Analyst Programme. For students interested in banking, investment management, treasury, capital markets, sustainable finance, economics, or development finance, this programme provides exceptional practical experience within one of the world s most respected international financial institutions. Click HERE to learn more about the World Bank Treasury Summer Internship 2027 Application Deadline Applications for the World Bank Treasury Summer Internship 2027 are currently open. The official announcement does not specify a closing deadline for applications. Interested students are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible through the official World Bank careers portal. Conclusion The World Bank Treasury Summer Internship 2027 offers an exceptional opportunity for talented undergraduate students to gain hands-on experience in international finance while contributing to the mission of one of the world s leading development institutions. Through a structured 10-week programme in Washington, DC, interns rotate across Treasury departments, receive mentorship from experienced professionals, participate in technical and leadership development sessions, and complete meaningful projects that strengthen both their technical expertise and professional skills. Beyond offering competitive compensation and visa sponsorship for eligible international participants, the internship also provides a direct pathway into the World Bank Treasury Junior Analyst Programme, making it an ideal opportunity for students seeking long-term careers in finance, investment, treasury operations, and international development. Eligible applicants should prepare their application materials carefully and apply as early as possible to maximize their chances of joining this highly selective programme. Discover more global Internship opportunities and Summer programs on OFY.
